text:
🔭 Context maintenance best practices

How to keep a living wiki / knowledge base current when the project underneath it constantly evolves.

TL;DR: Wikis die when treated as artifacts. Four things prevent rot: (1) atomic knowledge units — one idea per note, independently updatable; (2) mutation + dating over immutability — capture how context evolves; (3) embed context into workflow — surface decisions where work happens, not in a buried wiki; (4) for AI-assisted contexts, LLMs handle interconnection while humans curate accuracy.

Key patterns: ADRs (Architecture Decision Records, Nygard 2011) — append-only decision docs with supersession links; Evergreen notes (Matuschak) — atomic, standalone, linked; Diátaxis (Procida) — 4-type taxonomy matching update cadence to content type; ROT prevention with scheduled ownership + review cycles.

For the Personal OS: rewrite-in-place READMEs + provenance links already implement the core patterns. Gap: no ADR-style decision log for the OS — skill/automation design choices are implicit in CLAUDE.md without dates or supersession links.
